sections_cited: Sections 194-A and 194-B of the Customs Act

chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Constitution Petition No. D- 5099 of 2020 _________________________________________________________ Date Order with signature of Judge _________________________________________________________ Hearing of Case. 1. For hearing of CMA No. 21974/2020. 2. For Regular hearing. -------------- 13.09.2024. Ms. Dil Khurram Shaheen, Advocate for Petitioner. Mr. Khalid Mehmood Rajpar, Advocate for Respondent. Mr. Kashif Nazeer, Assistant Attorney General. ********* 1&2. This is a petition seeking implementation of an order passed by the Customs Appellate Tribunal, at Karachi. It appears that vide order dated 09.8.2024 passed by the Hon’ble Supreme Court1, whereby it has been held the Customs Appellate Tribunal has the power to execute orders passed by it while exercising jurisdiction under Sections 194-A and 194-B of the Customs Act, 1969, and consequently, since an adequate remedy is provided by law, the writ jurisdiction of the High Court cannot be invoked for executing orders passed by the Tribunal. In view of such position, this Petition is dismissed as not maintainable with pending applications, whereas the Petitioner is at liberty to seek appropriate remedy as observed in the above order of the Hon’ble Supreme Court. JUDGE JUDGE Ayaz 1 in Civil Petition No.3391/2024 [Khalid alias Muhammad Khalid and others v. Collector of Customs (Adjudication), Customs House, Lahore, etc.],
